# Questions in Livestream 142

Live Stream #142: Think About the Questions!!!

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp. The links use HTML anchors so GitHub can render them with `target="_blank"`.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fVPZXw6Vbt0?t=447" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">7:27</a> | Has later research materially changed the Egyptian loanword argument for the historicity of the Exodus? | He says no. New work has added detail, but it has not materially altered the overall argument. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fVPZXw6Vbt0?t=584" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">9:44</a> | What is the gift of tongues in 1 Corinthians 14, and are they angelic tongues? | He treats tongues as real language, not gibberish; in Second Temple thought that could include angelic language, while "mysteries" are speech not understood by ordinary hearers. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fVPZXw6Vbt0?t=1504" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">25:04</a> | Is gluttony only sinful in scarcity situations, or is overeating part of it? | He distinguishes overeating from gluttony, saying biblical gluttony is taking more than one's share in a way that harms others. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fVPZXw6Vbt0?t=2022" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">33:42</a> | How do you correct someone who rejects all scholarship as man-made doctrine? | He says you probably will not break through if the person rejects every tool of learning; even an English Bible already depends on scholarship. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fVPZXw6Vbt0?t=2407" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">40:07</a> | Did Moses's Egyptian name originally include a divine element that was later omitted? | He says that is possible; Egyptian names often had theophoric elements, and such elements could be shortened or dropped. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fVPZXw6Vbt0?t=3066" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">51:06</a> | How much would someone have to donate for a historical critique of Assassin's Creed Origins? | He jokes about setting a high price, then explains that archaeological games feel like work rather than relaxation now. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fVPZXw6Vbt0?t=3791" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:03:11</a> | Do marriages have to be instigated by a priest or pastor? | No. He says Scripture does not require a pastor or priest to conduct the ceremony, even though clergy often represent God's blessing in church tradition. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fVPZXw6Vbt0?t=4107" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:08:27</a> | What bad theories get unwarranted promotion because of perceived market value or public image? | He names ancient-alien pyramid claims and flat-earth arguments as examples of fringe ideas that get far more cultural traction than they deserve. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fVPZXw6Vbt0?t=4566" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:16:06</a> | Why does the Bible not mention the Anatolian Hittite Empire? | He says the empire had already collapsed by the time Israel entered the land, so the Bible encounters later Neo-Hittite states instead. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fVPZXw6Vbt0?t=5220" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:27:00</a> | Is there value in Manetho? | Yes, but cautiously. He says Manetho is useful while also being late and preserved only through later writers whose copies often disagree. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fVPZXw6Vbt0?t=5491" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:31:31</a> | Did prophets speak in tongues when communicating with God or angelic beings? | He says the text does not tell us; prophets were brought into God's presence, where the specific language is not the main issue. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fVPZXw6Vbt0?t=5585" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:33:05</a> | Are the ten kings in Revelation 17:12 the ten Roman provincial governors under Nero? | He is skeptical of that preterist identification because the passage says the kings receive authority with the beast, not merely before Nero. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fVPZXw6Vbt0?t=7154" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:59:14</a> | Why is the Mount Ebal curse tablet rejected, and is it valid in any case? | He says the problems are dating and epigraphy: the scans did not clearly show the claimed writing, so calling it a curse tablet is not secure. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fVPZXw6Vbt0?t=8205" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:16:45</a> | What would you recommend for someone seeking formal biblical education while working and raising children? | He says formal schooling is still better than self-teaching for a formal credential, but family obligations remain the priority and narrow what is realistic. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fVPZXw6Vbt0?t=8857" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:27:37</a> | Is there evidence outside the Bible that the apostles were martyred? | He says almost all of the evidence is extra-biblical, found in church histories and other later sources rather than in Acts itself. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fVPZXw6Vbt0?t=9643" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:40:43</a> | What do you think of R. C. Sproul's work? | He calls Sproul a strong Reformed teacher, especially on Reformation and New Testament topics, but says his Old Testament handling had weaknesses. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fVPZXw6Vbt0?t=10079" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:47:59</a> | If it was not a rib that God took from Adam, what was it? | He says the Hebrew term has a broad semantic range around "side" or torso, so the traditional "rib" rendering is not the only possible nuance. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fVPZXw6Vbt0?t=10917" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:01:57</a> | What is your view of E. A. Wallis Budge's work as an Egyptologist? | He says Budge popularized Egyptian material and produced impressive editions, but his translations were weak even by standards of his own time. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fVPZXw6Vbt0?t=11418" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:10:18</a> | What do you think of Budge's other works, such as translations of New Testament apocrypha and legendary texts? | He says Budge's Greek or Latin work may fare better, but his output varies because he sometimes translated with only cursory knowledge. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fVPZXw6Vbt0?t=11755" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:15:55</a> | Who counts as a legitimate Egyptologist? | He says the standard depends on era, but today a PhD in Egyptology and recognition by other Egyptologists are the usual marks. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fVPZXw6Vbt0?t=12191" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:23:11</a> | What were ancient Israelite family units like, and how would they view modern nursing homes and pushing kids out at 18? | He says Israelite clan life was multigenerational and wealth-preserving, so they would have seen that modern pattern as both cruel and wasteful. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fVPZXw6Vbt0?t=12572" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:29:32</a> | What language would Abraham and the Egyptians have communicated in, and did Egyptians use interpreters? | He says Abraham likely spoke Amorite, while Egyptian scribes and interpreters handled foreign-language diplomacy and correspondence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fVPZXw6Vbt0?t=12705" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:31:45</a> | What are your biggest problems with evolution? | He mentions concerns about circular dating claims and the difficulty of some proposed macroevolutionary sequences, while not adopting a standard creationist label. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fVPZXw6Vbt0?t=15554" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:19:14</a> | When should someone leave a church? | His answer is that you leave when God calls you elsewhere, and that can include leaving either a bad church or a good one for ministry reasons. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fVPZXw6Vbt0?t=16342" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:32:22</a> | What advice would you give people who want to become pastors, especially about sermon preparation? | He emphasizes language study, seminary preparation, and serious weekly work in the text rather than shallow paraphrase. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/fVPZXw6Vbt0?t=18075" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">5:01:15</a> | What do you think of Constantine the Great? | He says too much is made of Constantine: he was a Roman politician, not a pillar of faith, and his role at Nicaea is often overstated. |
